summary: Closed, unmerged test PR that was accidentally opened against doldecomp/melee instead of the author's fork. Although the diff was very large and touched CI, configure.py, orig/GALE01/sys, and 100+ source/header files across camera, fighter, item, stage, menu, library, toy, and VI systems, the only human comment says: "sry, was intended to go against my fork". Treat the code changes as non-authoritative for decomp knowledge.
what_changed: Changed GitHub Actions build workflow link-mode configure arguments by removing --require-protos from config_args in .github/workflows/build.yml.
Changed configure.py so --require-protos defaults to true and added a --no-require-protos flag to disable prototype enforcement.
Replaced orig/GALE01/sys/.gitkeep with a new symlink orig/GALE01/sys pointing at /tmp/melee_extract/sys.

Large source dump replaced many decomp stub comments with C implementations or partial implementations, including camera functions such as Camera_8002CB0C, Camera_8002CDDC, Camera_8002D85C, and Camera_80030E44 in src/melee/cm/camera.c.
Touched a broad set of subsystems: ftCommon Attack100/Guard/ItemThrow, Kirby init, item files, stage files, menu files, lb libraries, VI files, toy files, and headers/static headers.
PR was closed almost immediately and not merged; the author clarified it was intended to target their fork.
smart_moves: The configure.py flag design shown in the diff is a reusable CLI pattern: make the stricter behavior the default while providing an explicit --no-require-protos escape hatch.
The PR is useful as a process reminder: CI experiments and local extraction paths should be tested in forks or branches that cannot accidentally target upstream.
Because the PR was accidental and unreviewed, the smart project move was closing it rather than reviewing or merging a very large mixed-purpose diff.
decomp_lessons: Do not treat closed accidental PRs as validated decomp evidence, even when they contain many apparent function implementations.
Avoid mixing infrastructure changes, local orig extraction artifacts, headers, and large decomp source work in a single PR; it becomes difficult to review and unsafe to reuse.
Absolute local symlinks such as /tmp/melee_extract/sys are not portable repository artifacts and should not be committed to upstream.
Prototype enforcement is important enough in this project that CI/configure behavior around --require-protos needs deliberate review before being changed.

Large newly decompiled functions that rely on goto-heavy control flow, stack padding, and reordered locals should be reviewed in focused PRs with matching evidence; this PR provides no review confirmation.
naming_conventions: Existing placeholder symbol style used numeric names such as Camera_8002CB0C, Camera_8002CDDC, Camera_8002D85C, and Camera_80030E44.
Subsystem path and prefix conventions visible in touched files include cm/Camera_, ftCo_, ftKb_, gr*, it*, lb*, mn*, ty*, and vi*.
Static/internal declarations are organized with .static.h headers in examples such as lbaudio_ax.static.h, mplib.static.h, and vi0501.static.h.
No reviewer-approved renaming guidance was present in this PR.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The diff excerpt shows matching-oriented C patterns such as explicit gotos, manual temporary variables, PAD_STACK, signed 8-bit casts, pointer aliases to struct fields, and comments documenting stack offsets.
The camera code used direct field access into global camera state cm_80452C68 and camera globals cm_803BCCA0, plus explicit float math via sinf, cosf, tanf, and sqrtf.
The excerpt includes a note about checking controller input bits through 64-bit access, e.g. inputs.x18._u64 and inputs.x10._u64.
These tactics are observed in the diff but were not reviewed or accepted because the PR was accidental and unmerged.

review_feedback.author: itsgrimetime
review_feedback.text: sry, was intended to go against my fork
review_feedback.impact: Confirms the PR was accidental and should not be interpreted as an intentional upstream contribution.

searchable_terms: intended to go against my fork
follow_up_queries: Find later merged PRs that intentionally introduced --no-require-protos or changed prototype enforcement.
Find later accepted camera.c work for Camera_8002CB0C, Camera_8002CDDC, Camera_8002D85C, or Camera_80030E44 before reusing any implementation ideas from this accidental PR.
Check whether repository policy or CI later blocked absolute symlinks under orig/GALE01/sys.
Look for smaller reviewed PRs by itsgrimetime around the same branch or functions, since this PR was intended for a fork.
